chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper provides a link to its code repository: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/hash-mvba-7C57/. This explicitly satisfies the criteria for 2 points.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation is implemented using Python 3 and deployed on AWS EC2 t2.medium instances distributed across 13 AWS regions (WAN environment). This constitutes a physical testbed/large-scale deployment, qualifying for 2 points.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: The paper has a clear, explicit section (Section III.A) detailing the system model and adversary capabilities, specifically stating it considers an 'strongly adaptive and computationally bounded adversary' corrupting up to $f < n/5$ nodes. It formally defines the MVBA properties which serve as the basis for the fault model, qualifying for 2 points.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous, data-driven analysis of performance trade-offs by presenting Table II (Latency improvements) and Figure 3 (Throughput-latency trade-offs), quantifying latency reductions (e.g., 81% reduction) compared to prior work, qualifying for 2 points.

major_weakness: The paper's primary weakness is that it focuses heavily on theoretical complexity proofs rather than providing a comprehensive analysis of resource consumption metrics like CPU usage or energy consumption beyond latency and throughput.
